input.task:
Build a simple workflow with:
1. A Manual Trigger node
2. A Set node named "deny test"

The Set node doesn't need to set any specific fields — it can be empty or have a simple example field.
input.conversationContext: User wants a simple workflow with a Manual Trigger and a Set node called "deny test", then wants to run it.
input.reason: New simple workflow with manual trigger and a Set node — straightforward single-workflow build.

output.result: Error: `bypassPlan: true` is for edits to an EXISTING workflow and requires a `workflowId`. New workflow builds must go through `plan` so an orchestrator-run verification checkpoint is scheduled. Call `plan` with a `build-workflow` task instead.

input.summary: Build a minimal manual-trigger workflow with a Set node named 'deny test' and execute it.
assumptions: Set node will be named 'deny test' with no field modifications (passthrough), as no specific fields were requested

item.name: Deny Test
item.purpose: A simple workflow triggered manually that passes data through a Set node named 'deny test'. This serves as a minimal test workflow for manual execution.

item.title: Verify 'Deny Test' workflow runs successfully
item.instructions: Use verify-built-workflow with the work item ID from the wf-deny-test build outcome. Confirm the workflow executes without errors and the 'deny test' Set node appears in the output. Pass condition: execution completes with no errors.
